blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Not really. I been scared every day this week.
First time I said it out loud. Didn't plan to. It just came out in the car like it had been waiting.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm good. I can handle it.
He didn't push. But I felt the gap between what I said and what I meant.
I looked out the window. He let the silence sit. He was good at not filling it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What would you have done? At my age?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: Probably something dumb. And then something dumber to fix it.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: You know what I didn't have at your age?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: Somebody telling me I could walk away and still be a man.
He said it like it was simple. It wasn't simple. But it landed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: You don't owe that kid a fight. You don't owe him a reaction. You don't owe him anything. But you do owe yourself the good decision.
